books.title: Tales from Grimm
books.author: Wanda Gag
books.rationale: Mason had her youngest students read fairy tales from Grimm or Anderson. This retelling by Wanda Gag is gentler and less scary than many other translations, while still having good literary quality and simple English for students to practice reading themselves.

books.title: Around the World in 80 Tales
books.author: Saviour Pirotta
books.rationale: This book takes readers across six continents with short, entertaining folktales to stir our first graders' imaginations with tales from many lands.

books.title: Aesop's Favorite Fables
books.author: Aesop, illus. by Milo Winter
books.rationale: Aesop's Fables have made learning life's lessons enjoyable for 2500 years. The illustrations by Milo Winter set this collection apart.
books.maySubText: another collection of Aesop's Fables. Lesson plans include the story titles.

books.title: American Tall Tales
books.author: Mary Pope Osborne
books.rationale: These larger-than-life stories capture something of America's personality. Mary Pope Osborne has retold these stories well, and the wood-carved illustrations are delightful.
books.noteText: U.S. Student Option. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: The Spirit of Canada: Canada's Story in Legends, Fiction, Poems, and Songs
books.author: Barbara Hehner
books.rationale: Organized thematically, this beautiful anthology encompasses a wide variety of voices and stories that reflect Canada's past and present. It is an excellent accompaniment to Canadian history and literature, as it contains many different genres: poems, songs, letters, legends, stories, folk tales, historical accounts, and more.
books.noteText: Canadian students should purchase this book and NOT American Tall Tales for the Tall Tales/Fables course.
books.sharedText:
Canadian History: Grades 2-4
Tall Tales/ Fables

books.title: A Wonder Book for Girls and Boys
books.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
books.rationale: Nathaniel Hawthorne is a master storyteller. This collection of stories from Greek mythology is a great introduction for young children.
books.noteText: Grade 3 students should read EITHER Pilgrim's Progress OR A Wonder Book.
books.sharedText:
Mythology: Grade 2
Lit./Mythology: Grade 3

books.title: Pilgrim's Progress: Updated, Modern English
books.author: John Bunyan
books.rationale: Mason's students read the original text, and some Alveary members choose to do the same. However, we find this retelling robust and beautiful, without the archaic vocabulary of the original, making the ideas more accessible to young children.
books.noteText: Grade 3: Students should read EITHER Pilgrim's Progress OR A Wonder Book.
books.sharedText:
Classic Lit.: Grade 2
Lit./Mythology: Grade 3

books.title: A Wonder Book for Girls and Boys
books.author: Nathaniel Hawthorne
books.rationale: Nathaniel Hawthorne is a master storyteller. This collection of stories from Greek mythology is a great introduction for young children.
books.noteText: Grade 3 students should read EITHER Pilgrim's Progress OR A Wonder Book.
books.sharedText:
Mythology: Grade 2
Lit./Mythology: Grade 3

books.title: Pilgrim's Progress: Updated, Modern English
books.author: John Bunyan
books.rationale: Mason's students read the original text, and some Alveary members choose to do the same. However, we find this retelling robust and beautiful, without the archaic vocabulary of the original, making the ideas more accessible to young children.
books.noteText: Grade 3: Students should read EITHER Pilgrim's Progress OR A Wonder Book.
books.sharedText:
Classic Lit.: Grade 2
Lit./Mythology: Grade 3

books.title: Charlotte's Web
books.author: E.B. White
books.rationale: The classic tale is about a spider, a pig, and much more. This book was chosen not only for its enduring literary quality but also to provide opportunities in their lessons for young readers to read books themselves.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Heart of a Samurai
books.author: Margi Preus
books.rationale: Based on the true story of a Japanese boy who survived a shipwreck and was rescued by an American whaling ship, Heart of a Samurai functions as a transitional book between Grades 3-4. Starting in Grade 4, students study world history and read literature that increasingly coordinates with the time period. Set in both Japan and the US in the mid 19th century, this engaging narrative expands students' imaginations of the broader world which they will study in more detail in later years.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: In the Days of Giants
books.author: Abbie Farwell Brown
books.rationale: The Greeks were not the only ones with a story tradition that influenced Western culture. These Norse stories have also been highly influential, and Brown has retold them beautifully.

books.title: Russian Fairy Tales
books.author: Alexander Afanasyev
books.rationale: Pushkin, Afanasyev, and many others wrote and collected Russian folk tales in the 19th century. Students read a few tales in the lesson plans and may enjoy the others on their own time.
books.noteText: Although the free and hard copy editions are not identical, they are from the same translator with nearly identical language. The tales scheduled in the lesson plans are included in both texts and are only scheduled for a few weeks.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: South American Jungle Tales
books.author: Horacio Quiroga
books.rationale: A Uruguayan author and playwright who lived at the end of the 19th century, Quiroga is sometimes compared to Rudyard Kipling and Edgar Allen Poe.
books.noteText: Students only read a tale or two from this collection, so the free version is recommended.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: Short Stories & Folktales
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate and include additional folk tales and shorter writings from 19th century authors such as Tolstoy and George Macdonald.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4

books.title: The Tales of Uncle Remus: The Adventures of Brer Rabbit
books.author: Julius Lester
books.rationale: Whether he is besting Brer Fox or sneaking into Mr. Man's garden, Brer Rabbit demonstrates that brains are better than brawn, along with other life lessons. These classic tales from the African American tradition are full of humor and creativity, and Julius Lester's retelling makes them a delight for readers today.

books.title: The Lion, the Witch and the Wardrobe
books.author: C. S. Lewis
books.rationale: This piece of classic literature by C.S. Lewis was chosen to foster students' moral formation and love for a well-told story.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.maySubText: another in the Narnia series or a title in adjacent grades if students have already read The Lion, the Witch, and the Wardrobe

books.title: Hans Brinker or The Silver Skates
books.author: Mary Mapes Dodge
books.rationale: Mary Mapes Dodge was recognized as one of the leading authors of children's literature during the 19th century, and Hans Brinker is her most famous work. With themes of perseverance and selflessness, Hans Brinker's heartwarming tale of two siblings delights, instructs, and expands the imagination.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grade 4
General Lit.: Grades 5-6

books.title: The Age of Fable
books.author: Thomas Bulfinch
books.rationale: The Age of Fable contains mythological stories primarily from Greece and Rome, although it also includes some stories from Norse and Eastern traditions. This is a stretch book for Grades 5-6 as the reading level is more challenging than the mythology stories assigned in the earlier grades.

books.title: Russian Fairy Tales
books.author: Alexander Afanasyev
books.rationale: Pushkin, Afanasyev, and many others wrote and collected Russian folk tales in the 19th century. Students read a few tales in the lesson plans and may enjoy the others on their own time.
books.noteText: Although the free and hard copy editions are not identical, they are from the same translator with nearly identical language. The tales scheduled in the lesson plans are included in both texts and are only scheduled for a few weeks.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.noteText: Students only read a tale or two from this collection, so the free version is recommended.
books.sharedText:
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: Short Stories & Folktales
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate and include additional folk tales and shorter writings from 19th century authors such as Tolstoy and George Macdonald.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6
Mythology/Hist. Fiction: Grade 4

books.title: A Christmas Carol
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.rationale: As a famous work of 19th century literature, A Christmas Carol expands students' understanding of this historical period while also nourishing them with a thought-provoking tale about greed and generosity. It is scheduled in the mythology stream as Dicken's work has become a modern myth, shaping our conceptions of Christmas and more.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.rationale: A beloved piece of 19th century literature, this story follows four sisters as they grow and learn to live full and happy lives. The story is loosely set during the American Civil War and has remained popular since it was first published.
books.noteText:
Grades 7-8: Choose this OR Sir Gibbie.
Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 5-6

books.title: Hans Brinker or The Silver Skates
books.author: Mary Mapes Dodge
books.rationale: Mary Mapes Dodge was recognized as one of the leading authors of children's literature during the 19th century, and Hans Brinker is her most famous work. With themes of perseverance and selflessness, Hans Brinker's heartwarming tale of two siblings delights, instructs, and expands the imagination.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grade 4
General Lit.: Grades 5-6

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Man Who Invented Christmas directed
books.author: Bharat Nalluri
books.rationale: Movie based on the biographical novel by Les Standiford telling how A Christmas Carol was written.

books.title: A Christmas Carol
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.rationale: As a famous work of 19th century literature, A Christmas Carol expands students' understanding of this historical period while also nourishing them with a thought-provoking tale about greed and generosity. It is scheduled in the mythology stream as Dicken's work has become a modern myth, shaping our conceptions of Christmas and more.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
Lit./Mythology: Grade 5-6

books.title: Short stories and other writings
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate a selection of writings from a variety of 19th century authors such as Pushkin, O.Henry, Tagore, and others to give students exposure to a broader range of genres, authors, and ideas.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Little Women
books.author: Louisa May Alcott
books.rationale: A beloved piece of 19th century literature, this story follows four sisters as they grow and learn to live full and happy lives. The story is loosely set during the American Civil War and has remained popular since it was first published.
books.noteText:
Grades 7-8: Choose this OR Sir Gibbie.
Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 5-6

books.title: Sir Gibbie
books.author: George MacDonald
books.rationale: Although less well known than some of his friends, George Macdonald cast an outsized influence on his contemporaries and many 20th century writers who followed him, including C.S. Lewis and J.R.R. Tolkein. Macdonald's genius for portraying goodness and inspiring readers is on full display in this rags-to-riches tale of a mute boy from the Scotch Highlands.
books.noteText: Choose this OR Little Women.

books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.noteText: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.rationale: A famous gothic novel first published in 1818 and then revised in 1831, Frankenstein influenced the development of science fiction. This gripping story was composed as a result of a writing challenge when the author was only 19, and it revolves around personhood, conscience, and the ethics of scientific ambition, themes which are especially important for students to wrestle with in today's age of AI.

books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.noteText: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Souls of Black Folk
books.author: W.E.B. Dubois
books.rationale: This landmark text of American literature and sociology is paired with Up from Slavery by Booker T. Washington in the lesson plans. This pairing immerses students in the dialogues around the turn of the century as they read about Washington's life and aspirations and then consider Dubois's response.
books.noteText: Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Up from Slavery
books.author: Booker T. Washington
books.rationale: This is a first-person account of what it was like to be born on a slave plantation, live through the Civil War and Reconstruction, and make a new life as a free person. Washington's accomplishments and sense of hope are inspiring, and his founding of the Tuskegee Institute which continues as a historically black university and National Historic Site today is a remarkable achievement. Lesson plans pair Dubois's response to Washington to allow students a richer experience of the discussions of the times.
books.noteText:
Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.

Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Silas Marner
books.author: George Eliot (Mary Ann Evans)
books.rationale: Students spend two terms lingering with this nineteenth-century novel which depicts British village life while stretching the reader's moral imagination. Composition lessons build on these lessons by asking students to analyze characters, compare characters’ development during the two time periods of the novel, and use the events of the novel to discuss ethical questions.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Short stories and other writings
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate a selection of writings from a variety of 19th century authors such as Pushkin, O.Henry, Tagore, and others to give students exposure to a broader range of genres, authors, and ideas.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: English Literature for Boys and Girls
books.author: H.E. Marshall
books.rationale: A delightful introduction to the writers of English literature whose works hold the greatest appeal for the youthful reader. The life and personality of each author is given in outline, with enough material quoted from key works to give an idea of the text.
books.noteText: Selections used across multiple rotations.

books.title: History of Literature PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Curated selections of readings on literary developments, significant authors, and primary sources from the 19th century. Students use this PDF along with selected links and readings in Marshall's and Long's spines to deepen their relationship with 19th-century literature.

books.title: American Literature
books.author: William Long
books.rationale: A helpful chronological look at American Literature and authors.
books.noteText: Selections used across multiple rotations.

books.title: The Tempest (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Tempest
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This play is often called a Tragicomedy because it has both comedic and tragic elements. It is about an overthrown Duke and his daughter who are exiled on an island. The play addresses the themes of power, betrayal, magic, slavery, and the restoration of relationships.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: Henry IV Part 2 (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: Henry IV Part 2
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This history play is a tale about Prince Hal falling into his father's footsteps as king. Hal's life will no longer be the same when he feels the weight of the crown.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Winter's Tale
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: The Winter's Tale is play divided into two halves—tragedy and redemption. It begins in the court of Leontes, King of Sicilia, whose sudden and irrational jealousy leads him to accuse his wife Hermione of betrayal. His actions cause devastating consequences, including the loss of family, friendship, and trust. This play emphasizes the power of jealousy to destroy, but the greater power of love to restore.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Winter's Tale (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Making of a Poem
books.author: Eavan Boland, Mark Strand
books.rationale: Students read this anthology to learn how to study the characteristics and history of various poetic forms and then enjoy a number of examples from poets across the years, including many from this year's time period.

books.title: Poetry
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans draw in additional poetry from the 19th century to broaden the student's encounter with poetry from this time period.
books.maySubText: an anthology of 19th century poetry such as the one used in younger grades.

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Crescent Moon
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.rationale: This delightful collection of children's poetry was translated from Bengali into English by Tagore himself. Lesson plans will draw from this collection and include links to additional poetry by this famous 19th century author.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 1-4
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Aeneid
books.author: Virgil
books.rationale: The famous epic poem in which Virgil seeks to outdo Homer and build the glory of Rome. This text complements this year's studies in ancient history which cover the Roman period.
books.noteText: Excellent audiobook is also available on Amazon. Available on Learning Ally with Audio.

books.title: Jump Back, Paul: The Life and Poems of Paul Laurence Dunbar
books.author: Sally Derby
books.rationale: An engaging narrative telling about the life and times of Paul Laurence Dunbar, one of the most widely acclaimed American poets at the end of the 19th century. Dunbar's poetry is woven throughout the narrative so students can understand its context and significance.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7

Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Word Made Fresh
books.author: Abram Van Engen
books.rationale: Van Engen lays out a beautiful relational approach to poetry which resonates deeply with Mason. As he models a love for poetry and gives insights into how to enjoy, understand, and read poetry (or start on the journey!), students will continue growing their own delight. Lesson plans pair readings from the book with practical exercises.
books.noteText: The first section of the book (the more practical part) is scheduled in the lesson plans, but we recommend the whole book - which concludes with a theological reflection on the value of poetry - as a resource for parents and teachers.
books.sharedText:
Poetry Study: Grade 10
Poetry Study: Grade 11

books.title: Poetry Anthology
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Students use a poetry anthology of their choice (in print or online) for the exercises accompanying readings in The Word Made Fresh. Suggestions are listed in Extra Helpings for the course, but any anthology with a wide variety of poems and poets is sufficient.
books.sharedText:
Poetry Study: Grade 10
Poetry Study: Grade 11

books.title: Jump Back, Paul: The Life and Poems of Paul Laurence Dunbar
books.author: Sally Derby
books.rationale: An engaging narrative telling about the life and times of Paul Laurence Dunbar, one of the most widely acclaimed American poets at the end of the 19th century. Dunbar's poetry is woven throughout the narrative so students can understand its context and significance.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: Narrative of the Life of Frederick Douglass
books.author: Frederick Douglass
books.rationale: Students read extended selections of this classic 19th century narrative which details the author's story from slavery to freedom. For US students, this text will enhance their reading of Lincoln & Douglass in US History.
books.noteText: GRADES 9+ should choose this OR Up From Slavery and Souls of Black Folk.
books.sharedText:
General Lit: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Souls of Black Folk
books.author: W.E.B. Dubois
books.rationale: This landmark text of American literature and sociology is paired with Up from Slavery by Booker T. Washington in the lesson plans. This pairing immerses students in the dialogues around the turn of the century as they read about Washington's life and aspirations and then consider Dubois's response.
books.noteText: Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Up from Slavery
books.author: Booker T. Washington
books.rationale: This is a first-person account of what it was like to be born on a slave plantation, live through the Civil War and Reconstruction, and make a new life as a free person. Washington's accomplishments and sense of hope are inspiring, and his founding of the Tuskegee Institute which continues as a historically black university and National Historic Site today is a remarkable achievement. Lesson plans pair Dubois's response to Washington to allow students a richer experience of the discussions of the times.
books.noteText:
Choose The Souls of Black Folk with Up from Slavery OR Narrative of a Life of Frederick Douglas.

Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: The Count of Monte Cristo
books.author: Alexandre Dumas, translated and edited by Lowell Bair
books.rationale: A sweeping tale of (in)justice and revenge inspired by a real-life case, this historical adventure novel became one of Alexandre Dumas's most popular works.
books.noteText: Many different editions of this book exist, so if buying used be sure you are purchasing the Bantam Classics edition translated and abridged by Lowell Bair. Bair's version shortens the original work to a (somewhat) more manageable length without truncating the richness of the language and plot. If interested in reading the full text, we recommend Robin Buss's translation published by Penguin.

books.title: Jane Eyre
books.author: Charlotte Brontë, edited by Karen Swallow Prior
books.rationale: A coming of age story narrated in first person and set in 19th century England. Students experience the world through Jane's eyes and wrestle along with her when she faces various events and quandaries.
books.noteText:
This edition includes the full text along with reflection questions and an introduction. Since the lesson plans refer to this additional content, students should use this edition if possible.  Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose Jane Eyre OR James. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

books.title: James
books.author: Percival Everett
books.rationale: Recently published and already widely acclaimed, James retells Twain's Adventures of Huckleberry Finn from Jim's point of view. Funny and intense by turns and always insightful, Everett immerses students in 19th-century America. Alveary lessons pair selections of Huckleberry Finn alongside James to enrich students' understanding of the texts and times.
books.noteText:
Only recommended for mature 11-12th grade students. Due to it's length, students have the option to finish this book on their own time after end of term.

Choose James OR Jane Eyre. Students more interested in American Lit should consider James. Those more interested in British Lit should consider Jane Eyre.

books.title: Short stories and other writings
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans curate a selection of writings from a variety of 19th century authors such as Pushkin, O.Henry, Tagore, and others to give students exposure to a broader range of genres, authors, and ideas.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
General Lit.: Grades 9-10
General Lit.: Grades 11-12

books.title: Things Fall Apart
books.author: Chinua Achebe
books.rationale: Widely considered to be the foundational text of modern African fiction, Things Fall Apart provides a detailed, unflinching depiction of the complex life and beliefs of the Igbo tribe of Nigeria in the late 1800s, before and after their first encounters with European missionaries and the establishment of a colonial government. The story traces the life of the warrior Okonkwo: his upbringing and adulthood, his rise to prominence and and tragic fall, and finally his banishment and return to find his home forever changed. Written in English from an authentically African perspective, Achebe's novel directly challenges Western narratives of colonialism and stereotypes of Africa, yet eschews any simple resolutions.
books.noteText: Recommended for mature Grades 11-12 students due to sensitive subject matter. Students who might need a lighter read may choose a title from the Grades 9-10 literature selections.

books.title: English Literature for Boys and Girls
books.author: H.E. Marshall
books.rationale: A delightful introduction to the writers of English literature whose works hold the greatest appeal for the youthful reader. The life and personality of each author is given in outline, with enough material quoted from key works to give an idea of the text.
books.noteText: Selections used across multiple rotations.

books.title: History of Literature PDF
books.author: Alveary
books.rationale: Curated selections of readings on literary developments, significant authors, and primary sources from the 19th century. Students use this PDF along with selected links and readings in Marshall's and Long's spines to deepen their relationship with 19th-century literature.

books.title: American Literature
books.author: William Long
books.rationale: A helpful chronological look at American Literature and authors.
books.noteText: Selections used across multiple rotations.

books.title: The Tempest (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Tempest
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This play is often called a Tragicomedy because it has both comedic and tragic elements. It is about an overthrown Duke and his daughter who are exiled on an island. The play addresses the themes of power, betrayal, magic, slavery, and the restoration of relationships.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: Henry IV Part 2 (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: Henry IV Part 2
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: This history play is a tale about Prince Hal falling into his father's footsteps as king. Hal's life will no longer be the same when he feels the weight of the crown.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Winter's Tale
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: The Winter's Tale is play divided into two halves—tragedy and redemption. It begins in the court of Leontes, King of Sicilia, whose sudden and irrational jealousy leads him to accuse his wife Hermione of betrayal. His actions cause devastating consequences, including the loss of family, friendship, and trust. This play emphasizes the power of jealousy to destroy, but the greater power of love to restore.
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Winter's Tale (Audio recording)
books.author: Arkangel
books.sharedText:
Shakespeare: Grades 5-8
Plays: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Word Made Fresh
books.author: Abram Van Engen
books.rationale: Van Engen lays out a beautiful relational approach to poetry which resonates deeply with Mason. As he models a love for poetry and gives insights into how to enjoy, understand, and read poetry (or start on the journey!), students will continue growing their own delight. Lesson plans pair readings from the book with practical exercises.
books.noteText: The first section of the book (the more practical part) is scheduled in the lesson plans, but we recommend the whole book - which concludes with a theological reflection on the value of poetry - as a resource for parents and teachers.
books.sharedText:
Poetry Study: Grade 10
Poetry Study: Grade 11

books.title: Poetry Anthology
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Students use a poetry anthology of their choice (in print or online) for the exercises accompanying readings in The Word Made Fresh. Suggestions are listed in Extra Helpings for the course, but any anthology with a wide variety of poems and poets is sufficient.
books.sharedText:
Poetry Study: Grade 10
Poetry Study: Grade 11

books.title: Poetry
books.author: Various
books.rationale: Lesson plans draw in additional poetry from the 19th century to broaden the student's encounter with poetry from this time period.
books.maySubText: an anthology of 19th century poetry such as the one used in younger grades.

books.title: The Lightning Dreamer: Cuba's Greatest Abolitionist
books.author: Margarita Engle
books.rationale: This book is a poetic fictionalized account of famous Cuban abolitionist and author Gertrudis Gómez Avellaneda (1814-1873). Since Avellaneda wrote in Spanish, this biography allows students to get a glimpse of a significant 19th century author outside the English canon. Engle's style also provides opportunities for students to consider the differences between poetry and prose and experiment with point of view in connected composition assignments.
books.noteText: Available on Learning Ally with Audio.
books.sharedText:
Lit./Mythology: Grades 7-8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.title: The Crescent Moon
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.rationale: This delightful collection of children's poetry was translated from Bengali into English by Tagore himself. Lesson plans will draw from this collection and include links to additional poetry by this famous 19th century author.
books.sharedText:
Poetry: Grades 1-4
Poetry: Grades 5-6
Poetry Reading: Grade 7
Poetry Reading: Grade 8
Poetry Reading: Grades 9-12

books.author: Virgil

books.title: A Midsummer Night's Dream
books.author: William Shakespeare
books.rationale: One of Shakespeare’s most popular plays, A Midsummer Night’s Dream is a comedy set in Greece during the 12th century. The magical setting of this play is the city of Athens and the nearby enchanted woods where fairies and their mischief reign in the moonlight.
books.noteText: This is a good play for those who are newer to Shakespeare.

books.title: Jump Back, Paul: The Life and Poems of Paul Laurence Dunbar
books.author: Sally Derby
books.rationale: An engaging narrative telling about the life and times of Paul Laurence Dunbar, one of the most widely acclaimed American poets at the end of the 19th century. Dunbar's poetry is woven throughout the narrative so students can understand its context and significance.
